---
title: "Fig4C_confusionMatrix"
output: html_document
date: "2025-07-15"
---
  
  
```{r}
library(forcats)
library(caret)

### Fig 4C ###

cm_wel <- readRDS("../objects/fig4c_Rdata.rds")
colnames(cm_wel$table)<-c("Endometriosis","Control")
rownames(cm_wel$table)<-c("Endometriosis","Control")
cm_wel


draw_confusion_matrix <- function(cm) {
  
  layout(matrix(c(1,1,2)), heights = c(2, 1))
  par(mar=c(2,2,6,2)) 
  plot(c(140, 345), c(300, 450), type = "n", xlab="", ylab="", xaxt='n', yaxt='n')
  mtext('Confusion Matrix', side=3, line=1, cex=2) 
  
  # create the matrix 
  rect(150, 430, 240, 370, col='#E63E6B')
  text(195, 435, 'Endometriosis', cex=1.5)
  rect(250, 430, 340, 370, col='#10a9e5')
  text(295, 435, 'Control', cex=1.5)
  text(125, 370, 'Predicted', cex=2, srt=90, font=2)
  text(245, 450, 'Actual', cex=2, font=2)
  rect(150, 305, 240, 365, col='#10a9e5')
  rect(250, 305, 340, 365, col='#E63E6B')
  text(140, 400, 'Endometriosis', cex=1.5, srt=90)
  text(140, 335, 'Control', cex=1.5, srt=90)
  
  # add in the cm results 
  res <- as.numeric(cm$table)
  text(195, 400, res[1], cex=3, font=2, col='white')
  text(195, 335, res[2], cex=3, font=2, col='white')
  text(295, 400, res[3], cex=3, font=2, col='white')
  text(295, 335, res[4], cex=3, font=2, col='white')
  
  # add in the specifics 
  plot(c(100, 0), c(100, 0), type = "n", xlab="", ylab="", main = "Statistics", cex.main = 2,xaxt='n', yaxt='n')
  text(10, 65, names(cm$byClass[1]), cex=2, font=2)
  text(10, 20, round(as.numeric(cm$byClass[1]), 3), cex=1.5)
  text(50, 65, names(cm$byClass[2]), cex=2, font=2)
  text(50, 20, round(as.numeric(cm$byClass[2]), 3), cex=1.5)

  # add in the accuracy information 
  text(90, 65, names(cm$overall[1]), cex=2, font=2)
  text(90, 20, sprintf("%.3f", cm$overall[1]), cex=1.5)
}  

#png(filename = "MLmatrix_fig4C.png", res=600,width=200, height=160, units='mm')
draw_confusion_matrix(cm_wel)
#dev.off()
```
